title: Network Interceptor
module_title: Man-in-the-Middle Attacks and Network Credential Capture
learning_objectives: Execute ARP spoofing to intercept network traffic
Capture and extract credentials from network protocols
Demonstrate NTLM hash capture using Responder
Perform DNS spoofing for credential harvesting
key_topics: ARP spoofing mechanics and tools (arpspoof, bettercap)
scenario: On the corporate LAN during an internal pentest. Demonstrate MitM attacks: ARP spoofing, credential capture, Responder hash capture, DNS spoofing.

scenario_context.setting: Internal corporate network with unencrypted protocols
scenario_context.challenge: Demonstrate network-layer attacks to highlight protocol security issues
scenario_context.relevance: Many organizations rely on network segmentation; shows internal threat model

subtasks.title: ARP Spoofing Setup
subtasks.description: Configure ARP spoofing in the lab.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Set up arpspoof between victim and gateway. Verify traffic redirects through attacker machine using tcpdump. Document ARP table changes.
subtasks.evidence_required: ARP spoof setup with traffic capture proof

subtasks.title: Credential Capture
subtasks.detailed_guidance: While ARP spoofing is active, capture traffic with Wireshark/tcpdump. Identify HTTP authentication packets. Extract base64-encoded credentials.
subtasks.evidence_required: Captured HTTP credentials with PCAP evidence

subtasks.title: Responder Hash Capture
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Deploy Responder on network to poison LLMNR and NBNS queries. Capture NTLM hashes from Windows hosts. Document hash types.
subtasks.evidence_required: Responder output with NTLM hashes captured

subtasks.title: DNS Spoofing Demo
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use dnsspoof or bettercap to intercept DNS queries and redirect to attacker-hosted phishing page. Document victim's interaction.

subtasks.title: Detection Analysis
subtasks.description: How could these be detected and prevented?
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Analyse logs for MitM indicators (ARP storms, DNS anomalies, NTLM failures). Document detection mechanisms and prevention strategies.
subtasks.evidence_required: Detection analysis report with IDS/monitoring recommendations

reflection_prompt: How would organizations segment networks to mitigate MitM attacks? What encryption protocols prevent these attacks?

quiz.question: Why is ARP spoofing effective on local networks?
quiz.explanation: ARP protocol lacks authentication, allowing attackers to send gratuitous ARP replies that update victim ARP tables.

quiz.question: What is the primary target of Responder LLMNR poisoning?
options: Windows hosts performing name resolution failures
quiz.explanation: When normal name resolution fails, Windows hosts fall back to LLMNR, which Responder poisons to capture NTLM hashes.

mentor_notes: Verify lab setup is isolated and ethical. Assess technical understanding of network protocols. Detection analysis should show depth of security thinking. Ethics compliance is critical.
